symptoms.symptom_name: Alternating paroxysmal tachycardia and severe bradycardia (tachy-brady)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Alternating tachy/brady with pauses is the defining feature of the tachycardia-bradycardia variant of sinus node dysfunction.
A reflex faint does not produce documented sustained tachycardia alternating with resting bradycardia.
Syncope from post-tachycardia sinus pauses marks haemodynamically significant sinus node disease.
Cardiac syncope in an elder mandates ECG/troponin to exclude ACS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronotropic incompetence — months of fatigue and exertional breathlessness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Failure to raise heart rate with exertion is a core manifestation of sinus node dysfunction and explains the chronic fatigue.
Paroxysmal atrial tachyarrhythmia is the tachycardic limb of the syndrome.

symptoms.symptom_name: Resting bradycardia HR ~42 with low-normal blood pressure (caregiver-measured)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symptomatic resting bradycardia; here it is a staff-reported spot pulse (admissible caregiver-obtained data), not a monitored rhythm.
Beta-blockade lowers the sinus rate and compounds an already failing node.
A recently started nodal-blocking drug is a reversible contributor that must be withheld.
Beta-blockade unmasks/worsens latent sinus node disease, precipitating symptomatic pauses.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain, diaphoresis or vomiting with the episodes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lowers ACS probability but does not exclude it in an elderly patient — silent ischaemia is common.
Non-specific; overlaps with low output from bradycardia and does not by itself indicate a benign cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prolonged sinus pauses / SA exit block on ECG or telemetry

investigation_details.expected_result: Sinus bradycardia with prolonged pauses; paroxysmal atrial tachyarrhythmia captured on monitoring
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The diagnosis and the pauses are never captured; the patient is left unmonitored at risk of asystole.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Capturing the pauses/exit block confirms sinus node dysfunction — the diagnostic gold standard, only obtainable in hospital.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ambulatory (Holter) rhythm monitoring capturing symptomatic pauses

investigation_details.expected_result: Symptom-rhythm correlation of pauses with presyncope/syncope
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Intermittent pauses missed on a single ECG go undocumented.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symptom-rhythm correlation confirms the pauses are the cause of syncope.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Alternating tachy/brady + syncope + chronotropic incompetence + palpitations together are the classic sinus node dysfunction constellation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line to characterise the rhythm, exclude complete heart block and screen for ischaemia; the AI cannot perform it and must escalate for it.
Captures pauses and protects against unwitnessed asystole during transfer.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Troponin and 12-lead ECG to exclude ACS
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Syncope in an elder requires ACS exclusion despite the absence of chest pain.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum electrolytes, renal function and TSH
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screen for reversible metabolic/thyroid contributors to bradyarrhythmia.
For symptom-rhythm correlation if the diagnosis is not captured on initial monitoring.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Carotid sinus massage or tilt-table testing at the bedside via telehealth
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Provocative testing is unsafe and inappropriate remotely in an unstable patient and must not be attempted over chat.
